title: Turkish Yogurt Chicken with Buttered Rice

subs.name: paneer (400 g) — skip searing beyond 2 minutes per side
Kashmiri chilli powder gives the colour without much heat
ghee — closer to the traditional taste than oil

steps.text: Whisk half the curd with garlic, lemon juice, half the paprika, pepper and salt. Coat the chicken and marinate 15 minutes on the counter (or up to overnight in the fridge).
Wash the rice and cook it with 2.5 cups water and a little salt — in a pot, rice cooker, or 2 whistles in a pressure cooker. Fluff with 1 tbsp of the butter.
Heat oil in a wide tawa or skillet on high. Lay the chicken strips in a single layer and sear without moving for 3 minutes until charred spots form, then turn and cook 4–5 minutes more until done.
In a small pan, melt the remaining butter with the chopped onion until soft. Add grated tomato and remaining paprika and simmer 3 minutes into a loose, brick-red butter sauce.
Plate rice, top with chicken, spoon over the remaining whisked curd, then drizzle the paprika butter on top. Eat immediately while the butter is still sizzling.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: butter → ghee — closer to the traditional taste than oil; chicken → paneer (400 g) — skip searing beyond 2 minutes per side; red-chilli-powder → Kashmiri chilli powder gives the colour without much heat
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Everything here is standard Indian grocery: curd instead of Turkish yogurt (hang it in a muslin cloth for 15 minutes so it doesn't split), Kashmiri chilli for paprika, and a tawa instead of a cast-iron grill. Ghee can replace butter.
